records.ety: [L. expellere, expulsum; ex out + pellere to drive: cf.F. expeller. See Pulse a beat.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Expelled (?), p. pr. & vb. n.. Expelling.]

defs: To drive or force out from that within which anything is contained, inclosed, or situated; to eject; as, to expel air from a bellows.
quotes: Did not ye . . . expel me out of my father's house?

defs: To drive away from one's country; to banish.
quotes: Forewasted all their land, and them expelled.
He shall expel them from before you . . . and ye shall possess their land.

defs: To cut off from further connection with an institution of learning, a society, and the like; as, to expel a student or member.
To keep out, off, or away; to exclude.

quotes: Then he another and another [shaft] did expel.
